text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is setting SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% in three months." To track progress, break the project into milestones, such as completing the first design draft. Each milestone leads to a deliverable, like a final report or a prototype. Finally, stakeholder alignment means everyone involved agrees on the goals and expectations. This avoids confusion and keeps the team focused on the same outcomes.
